content:
# Strategy

## Goal
Formalize the main results of Landesman–Litt, *Geometric local systems on very
general curves and isomonodromy* (MR4736527, arXiv:2202.00039): the rank bound
`thm:very-general-VHS` (an analytically very general $n$-pointed hyperbolic
genus-$g$ curve carries no non-isotrivial polarizable integral VHS of rank
$<2\sqrt{g+1}$) together with its corollaries
(`cor:geometric-local-systems`, `cor:abelian-schemes`, `cor:non-density`) and
the isomonodromy results they rest on (`thm:hn-constraints-parabolic`,
`cor:stable-parabolic`, `thm:counterexample`).

This is an **autoformalize-stage** project: the immediate deliverable is a
complete, dependency-correct blueprint plus faithful, type-correct Lean
signatures (proof bodies may be `sorry`). The blueprint carries the full
mathematical roadmap (informal proofs from the paper); the loop later discharges
the sorries.

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|-------|--------|-----------|-----|-------------------|-------|
| Foundations & main statements (Foundations.tex / Basic.lean) | ACTIVE | 2 | ~120–250 | curves, local systems / `π₁` reps, VHS (absent) | VHS & "geometric origin" have no Mathlib analogue → bespoke defs |
| Parabolic bundles (Parabolic.tex) | NEXT | 2 | ~150–300 | vector bundles on curves, `deg`/slope (partial) | parabolic structures fully bespoke; Serre duality for parabolic |
| Atiyah bundles & isomonodromy (Deformation.tex) | NEXT | 2 | ~150–300 | connections, `Ω¹`, `H¹` (partial) | isomonodromic deformation is analytic; model algebraically |
| Hodge-theoretic preliminaries (Hodge.tex) | NEXT | 2 | ~120–250 | Higgs bundles, Hodge bundles (absent) | curvature/positivity of Hodge bundles is hard analytically |
| Counterexample / Kodaira–Parshin (Counterexample.tex) | LATER | 1–2 | ~80–180 | finite covers, $S_d$-reps | center-free quotient group construction |
| HN-filtration analysis (HarderNarasimhan.tex) | LATER | 3–4 | ~250–500 | HN filtration, Clifford bound (absent) | technical heart; many interlocking lemmas |
| Hodge-theoretic results (HodgeResults.tex) | LATER | 2 | ~120–250 | discreteness/unitarity arguments | ties everything together; depends on all above |

LOC ranges are for faithful signatures + helper scaffolding; full sorry-free
proofs of the technical heart are a separate, much larger effort tracked by the
loop once statements are in place.

## Completed

| Phase | Iters (done@ · used) | LOC | Files | Key results | Reusable techniques | Pitfalls |
|-------|----------------------|-----|-------|-------------|---------------------|----------|
| — | — | — | — | (none yet) | — | — |

## Routes
Single route. The paper has one logical spine: geometric/Hodge consequences are
*reductions* to the isomonodromy/HN-filtration analysis (§5–§7 rest on §2–§4).
No alternative mathematical route is pursued; the only open choice is the
*modeling fidelity* of analytic objects (see Open questions). The unipotent
special case (replace parabolic stability with ordinary stability, `cor:stable`
in place of `cor:stable-parabolic`) is a documented simplification, not a
separate route.

## Open key strategic questions
- How faithfully to model analytic objects (complex orbifold, analytic moduli
  stack $\mathscr{M}_{g,n}$, "nearby curve", isomonodromic deformation) absent
  Mathlib support — abstract predicate vs. concrete construction.
- Whether to model VHS / "geometric origin" / character variety as bespoke
  structures or as opaque predicates carrying just the properties used.
- How much of parabolic-bundle theory (Serre duality, HN filtration) to build
  vs. axiomatize as Mathlib-style anchors during the autoformalize stage.

## Mathlib gaps & new material
Gaps to fill:
- Parabolic / coparabolic bundles, parabolic degree/slope, parabolic stability & HN filtration.
- Variations of Hodge structure (complex/integral, polarizable) and Higgs bundles.
- Clifford's theorem for vector bundles on curves; generic global generation bounds.
New project material:
- Hyperbolic pointed curve; analytically (very) general point of a complex orbifold.
- Flat vector bundle with regular singularities; isomonodromic deformation; "nearby curve".
- Local system of geometric origin; character variety; Atiyah / parabolic Atiyah bundle.
